description: All field types supported by Workfront Planning MCP tools. All types can be created as fields on a record type. The distinction below is whether record values are user-writable or auto-computed.

userWritableValueFields.description: Single-line plain text. Used as the primary field for record names.
userWritableValueFields.notes: The primary field (first field) is always text and required.

userWritableValueFields.description: Multi-line rich text with plain and HTML representations.
Rich text stored as HTML. Same value format as long-text.
Numeric value with configurable precision and sign.

configOptions.currency: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP). Default: USD.

userWritableValueFields.description: Date or datetime in ISO 8601 format.

configOptions.hasTime: Boolean. If true, include time component.

userWritableValueFields.description: Single value from a predefined list of options.

configOptions.options: Array of {displayName, color} objects. Colors: light-blue, dark-blue, light-cyan, dark-cyan, light-teal, dark-teal, light-green, dark-green, light-yellow, dark-yellow, light-orange, dark-orange, light-red, dark-red, light-pink, dark-pink, light-purple, dark-purple, light-gray, dark-gray.

userWritableValueFields.notes: When setting values, use the option 'name' (internal), not 'displayName'.

userWritableValueFields.description: Multiple values from a predefined list of options.

configOptions.options: Array of {displayName, color} objects. Same color palette as single-select.

userWritableValueFields.notes: Value is always an array of option names.

userWritableValueFields.description: Link to records in another (or the same) record type.

configOptions.referenceOptions.multiple: Boolean. Allow multiple linked records. Default: false.
configOptions.referenceOptions.backField: Provide to create a BIDIRECTIONAL link. Omit for unidirectional. Only displayName is required; multiple defaults to true. Type and recordTypeId are auto-filled by the server.
configOptions.referenceOptions.backField.displayName: Display name for the reverse field on the target record type (REQUIRED when backField is provided).
configOptions.referenceOptions.backField.multiple: Boolean. Whether the back-reference allows multiple links. Default: true.
configOptions.referenceOptions.externalOptions: Connection config for external record types (omit for native).

userWritableValueFields.notes: Values are ALWAYS arrays of record IDs, even for single references: ["Rc123"]. For bidirectional links, provide backField with a displayName — the server auto-sets createBackReference=true, type=reference, and recordTypeId=source record type.

userWritableValueFields.description: Link to one or more users (people field).

configOptions.referenceOptions.multiple: Boolean. Allow multiple users. Default: false.

userWritableValueFields.notes: Values are arrays of user objects: [{"id": "userId"}].

computedOrSystemFields.description: You define the formula expression on the field itself (via configOptions when creating/updating the field). Record values are then auto-computed by the server — you cannot set them directly. Read the docs://formula-syntax resource for syntax reference.

configOptions.returnType: One of: TEXT, NUMBER, PERCENTAGE, CURRENCY, DATE, DATETIME, TEXT_ARRAY.

computedOrSystemFields.description: A projection of a field value from the linked record. To change a lookup value, update the source record directly or change which record the reference field points to.
System field: user who created the record.
System field: timestamp when the record was created.
System field: user who last modified the record.
System field: timestamp of last modification.
System field: user who approved the record.
File attachment field. Not settable via MCP tools.
